  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 100,

  "sound_source": "bearing_inner_or_outer_race_defect",
  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 4.2,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Periodic, sharp impact-like clicking sounds indicating a localized defect on a bearing race or rolling element",
    "High-frequency ringing following each impact, characteristic of metal-on-metal contact from spalling or a crack"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Schedule immediate bearing replacement during the next planned maintenance window",
    "Monitor vibration levels and temperature of the bearing housing in the interim",
    "Ensure adequate lubrication is maintained to mitigate further rapid degradation"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals distinct, rhythmic, high-amplitude impact noises superimposed on the background machine bed noise. This signature is highly characteristic of a localized defect (such as spalling, pitting, or a crack) on the bearing's inner race, outer race, or rolling elements. The regularity of the impacts at a constant slow speed indicates a cyclic fault that requires intervention before catastrophic failure occurs.",
  "references": [
    "ISO 13373-3 (Condition monitoring and diagnostics of machines - Vibration condition monitoring)",
    "SKF Bearing Damage and Failure Analysis Guide"
  ]
}
